prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the text into exactly one of these categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Category definitions and rules:

- alarm_set: User requests setting a timer or alarm for a specific time or duration (e.g., "in 10 minutes", "at 6 am"). Distinguish from reminders about events/appointments (use calendar_set for those).
- calendar_set: Creating, scheduling, or setting a reminder for an event or appointment (e.g., meetings, trips, activities). Use for "remind me to..." when the context is an upcoming event/task, not just a timed alert.
- alarm_query: User asks about existing alarms or timers.
- calendar_query: User asks about upcoming events or their calendar/agenda.
- calendar_remove: Deleting or canceling a scheduled event from the calendar.
- general_quirky: Catch-all for queries, requests, or statements that do not fit any other category, including jokes, chit-chat, or unusual requests. Do NOT use for factual/celebrity queries—use qa_factoid or recommendation_events/locations as appropriate.
- qa_factoid: Requests for factual information about a person, place, thing, or event (e.g., "tell me about Rihanna", "has the celebrity done any charitable work"). Use this for factual lookup—not general_quirky.
- recommendation_locations: User asks for recommendations about places or locations to visit (e.g., "best hiking trail with waterfalls in the Smoky Mountains").
- play_audiobook: Request to play, resume, replay, or otherwise control audiobook playback (e.g., "replay The God of Small Things", "resume playback" when the last played audio was an audiobook). Do NOT confuse with play_music.
- play_music: Control or request for music tracks, albums, or playlists. Only use if the utterance clearly refers to music; otherwise, prefer play_audiobook for audiobook titles/commands.
- social_post: User wants to post or share content on a social network.
- qa_definition: User asks for a word, term, or concept definition (e.g., "what is X").
- For remaining categories, select only if the user intent directly matches their function.

Tie-breakers:
- If two categories seem possible, pick the more specific or action-oriented category.
- If unsure between play_music and play_audiobook, choose play_audiobook if the context or title matches a known book or the user refers to resuming/replaying content that can be an audiobook.
- If unsure between general_quirky and a factual/recommendation category, choose the factual/recommendation one.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the input text into exactly one of: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Category definitions and decision rules:

- alarm_set: User wants to set an alarm or timer for a specific time or interval (e.g., "set a timer for 10 minutes", "set alarm for 6 am"). Do NOT use for reminders/tasks/events—use calendar_set instead.
- alarm_query: User asks about existing alarms or timers.
- calendar_set: Create/schedule an event, meeting, appointment, or reminder (e.g., "remind me to...", "add yoga to my calendar"). Always use for reminders about upcoming activities or tasks, not simple countdowns/timed alerts (those go to alarm_set).
- calendar_query: Ask about upcoming or scheduled events, meetings, reminders, or appointments.
- calendar_remove: Cancel or delete a calendar event, meeting, or scheduled task/reminder.
- cooking_recipe: Any query or request for cooking instructions, recipes, or ingredients.
- datetime_query: Ask for the current date, time, day, holidays, or time-related info.
- email_query: Ask about existing emails or email settings, or request to read/edit/search for an email, EXCEPT actions where user wants to send a new message—those are email_sendemail.
- email_sendemail: User asks to send a new email message, or to compose/send (e.g., "email John", "send an email to").
- general_quirky: Requests/statements that don't fit any other category, e.g., chit-chat, jokes, or offbeat commands. Use only when no factual lookup or clear intent matches another category. Do NOT use for celebrity, person, place, event info (use qa_factoid), or for recommendations.
- iot_hue_lightoff: User requests to turn off one or more Hue lights/devices.
- lists_createoradd: Make a new list or add items to an existing list.
- lists_query: Ask about the contents of a list.
- lists_remove: Remove items from a list.
- music_query: User asks about music info (top songs, artists, albums, etc.) but does not explicitly request playback (do NOT use for "play", "start", etc.—that is play_music).
- play_music: User explicitly requests to play, start, stop, pause, resume, or control music playback (“play jazz”, “next track”, “pause music”).
- news_query: Ask for the news, headlines, or news about a topic.
- play_audiobook: Play, resume, or control an audiobook (use only if referring to audiobooks or resuming playback that could be an audiobook; prefer over play_music if the title is a known book).
- play_podcasts: Play or control podcast playback.
- play_radio: Play, stop, or change radio stations.
- qa_currency: Ask about currency values, conversion, or related facts.
- qa_definition: User asks for the definition or meaning of a word, term, phrase, or concept (keywords: "what is", "define", "meaning of").
- qa_factoid: Ask for factual information about a person, place, thing, or event (e.g., "tell me about X", "has Y done..."). Use for all celebrity or notable person lookups, and for factual "about" or "has ... done" questions.
- qa_stock: Ask about the value of stocks or shares, or information on a company’s stock.
- recommendation_events: User asks for recommendations of things to do (activities, concerts, festivals, etc.).
- recommendation_locations: User asks for places to visit or see, including best destinations, attractions, venues, or physical sites. Always use for “what’s the best place/trail/restaurant” queries.
- social_post: User asks to post, share, upload, or broadcast content to a social network/site.
- takeaway_order: Order food or request for food delivery/takeout.
- transport_query: Ask about public transport, travel times, car, bike, or ride-hailing info.
- weather_query: Ask about the weather, forecasts, or weather conditions.

Tie-breaker & disambiguation rules:
- For questions about the meaning or definition, use qa_definition; for all other factual lookups (esp. about people, places, things, events), use qa_factoid.
- For recommendations about places/locations, always use recommendation_locations, not general_quirky.
- For “tell me about” or “has X done Y” about a person/celebrity or place/event, use qa_factoid, not general_quirky.
- Use play_music only for explicit playback/controls; use music_query for music info requests with no playback intent.
- For reminder/task requests, use calendar_set unless the user requests a simple alert/timer (then use alarm_set).
- Prefer specific/action-oriented categories over general ones if more than one might apply.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
